text: I arrive as engineering manager evaluating team collaboration and security. My past: My team already uses several tools, and every platform decision creates migration and compliance risk.
Today I want to: Decide whether GitHub feels credible enough for a team evaluation.
My life goal is: Protect my team's delivery reputation by choosing stable engineering systems.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub

text: I see "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub".
I think: I see GitHub positioning itself as steady through change, which is relevant to my concern about platform risk.
My reaction: My first reaction is cautiously positive, but the title alone is not enough to prove credibility for a team evaluation.
Idea: show a clear path from the homepage to enterprise security, migration, and team governance proof.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month(s) is reasonable if the next step gets me closer to judging whether GitHub protects my team's delivery reputation.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that GitHub feels familiar and potentially credible, but I have low confidence until I see concrete security and collaboration evidence.
